plan: Trim SPY to 20% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and broad market exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single-name concentration.
Buy XLV at 8% to diversify into the healthcare sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting around 1.5% per month. It significantly de-risks the portfolio by eliminating leveraged ETFs and reducing concentration. Expect lower volatility, but also more limited upside potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to underperformance and high risk.
Trim SPAL to 2% to reduce leveraged ETF exposure.
Trim QQQI to 15% to rebalance towards growth.
Buy RTX at 10% to add a high-scoring, diversified long setup.
Buy AMAT at 8% for high-scoring semiconductor exposure, balancing SMH/SOXL trims.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting around 2.5% per month. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while introducing new high-quality setups. Expect moderate volatility and growth potential, with a focus on managing downside.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single-name concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its significant underperformance and downtrend.
Trim QQQI to 10% to free up capital for higher-growth opportunities.
Trim SMH to 8% due to its downtrend, reallocating to stronger setups.
Buy RTX at 15% to press a high-scoring, diversified long setup.
Buy AMAT at 12% to add a high-scoring semiconductor name.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth plan targets around 4% per month, leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining higher overall exposure. It will significantly increase both potential upside and drawdown risk. Be aware that leveraged ETFs, even if added, decay daily, and strict stop discipline is paramount to manage potential rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and allow for more income focus.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure entirely.
Increase QQQI to 35% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single-name exposure.
Sell SMH to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and potential reinvestment into income assets.
income.read: This income plan aims to park cash for yield, targeting around 1% per month through distributions. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income by rotating into income-focused ETFs and cutting high-beta names. While designed for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
